claims.label: Definition 1
claims.statement: The navigation state is x_k = [r_k, v_k, q_k, b_k, dot{b}_k]^T, combining translational state, attitude quaternion, clock bias, and clock drift for confidence diagnostics.

claims.label: Definition 2
claims.statement: Quaternionic frame residual is theta_delta = 2 arccos(|w_delta|) derived from q_delta = q_obs q_est^{-1}, interpreted as geodesic attitude mismatch on S^3 under sign-equivalent unit quaternion representation.

claims.label: Definition 3
claims.statement: Normalized innovation score is d_k^2 = e_k^T S_k^{-1} e_k with e_k = z_k - h(x_k), providing covariance-aware residual magnitude for measurement consistency checks.

claims.label: Definition 4
claims.statement: Navigation coherence score C_nav is a weighted combination of residual, frame, clock, observability, and dynamics component scores used to summarize confidence and support diagnostic labels.

claims.label: Proposition 1
claims.statement: Using |w_delta| in theta_delta = 2 arccos(|w_delta|) yields an angular mismatch that is invariant under unit-quaternion sign choice, because q and -q represent the same physical attitude.
claims.proof_sketch: Sign inversion negates all quaternion components, so scalar part sign flips but absolute value is preserved.

claims.label: Method claim
claims.statement: Composite PNT confidence scoring can classify residual patterns into diagnostic categories such as geometry weakness, timing degradation, or measurement anomalies when component terms are calibrated.
claims.notes: Proposed method claim; not an empirical result in this draft.

claims.label: Validation plan claim
claims.statement: Phase I simulation scenarios are proposed to test whether the coherence framework detects confidence degradation earlier and with better mode separation than residual-threshold-only checks.
claims.notes: Validation criteria are proposed only; no completed test campaign is reported.
